sourceMovie.title: The House Bunny
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The House Bunny for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Fred Wolf keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Kissing Booth
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The House Bunny clicked for you, The Kissing Booth (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The House Bunny?
faq.answer: Try The Kissing Booth, The Kissing Booth 2, Modern Times—each shares DNA with The House Bunny while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The House Bunny?
faq.answer: The House Bunny (2008) is best described as Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The House Bunny worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
